examples: Leaves develop chlorosis; stems are short and slender, and anthocyanin discoloration occurs on stems, petioles, and lower leaf surfaces.
Phosphorus-deficient plants are often stunted, with leaves turning a characteristic dark green, often with the accumulation of anthocyanin.
Smith says that some species, most famously maples, produce anthocyanin during autumn as a kind of antioxidant for trees.
The precise triggers for anthocyanin production could thus take years to figure out.
Where these signals intersect, the production of red anthocyanin pigments is induced.
